quiz.title: AP Calculus AB Quiz 8
quiz.subtitle: Applications of Derivatives — Motion, Rates, and Linear Approximation (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: The position of a particle moving along a line is given by $s(t) = 3t^2 - \ln t$, where $s$ is in feet and $t$ is in seconds ($t > 0$). Find the velocity of the particle at $t = 1$ second.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $f(x) = 4\sqrt{x}$ at the point where $x = 9$.
A cube is expanding. The edge length is increasing at a rate of 0.2 cm/s. How fast is the volume increasing when the edge length is 10 cm? (Volume $V = s^3$)
The cost (in dollars) to produce $x$ units is given by $C(x) = 5000 + 25x + 0.03x^2$. Find the marginal cost when $x = 200$.
A particle moves along a line with position $s(t) = t^3 - 12t^2 + 36t$. Find the velocity at $t = 5$ seconds. Is the particle moving forward or backward at that instant?
questions.explanation: Velocity $v(t) = s'(t) = 3t^2 - 24t + 36$. At $t = 5$: $v(5) = 75 - 120 + 36 = -9$ ft/s. Since $v(5) < 0$, the particle is moving backward (negative direction).

questions.question: Find the equation of the tangent line to $f(x) = \frac{1}{x^2}$ at the point $(2, 0.25)$. Use it to estimate the value of $\frac{1}{(2.1)^2}$.
Using the position function $s(t) = 3t^2 - \ln t$, find the acceleration of the particle at $t = 2$ seconds.
questions.explanation: Velocity $v(t) = s'(t) = 6t - t^{-1}$. Acceleration $a(t) = v'(t) = 6 + t^{-2} = 6 + \frac{1}{t^2}$. At $t = 2$: $a(2) = 6 + \frac{1}{4} = 6.25$ ft/s².

questions.question: The temperature of a cup of coffee (in °F) is given by $T(t) = 70 + 120e^{-0.1t}$, where $t$ is in minutes. Find the instantaneous rate of cooling at $t = 10$ minutes.
A rectangle has a constant area of 50 cm². The length is increasing at a rate of 2 cm/s. How fast is the width changing when the length is 10 cm? (Area $A = l \times w$)
questions.explanation: $A = l \times w = 50$ (constant). Differentiate: $0 = l\frac{dw}{dt} + w\frac{dl}{dt}$. Solve: $\frac{dw}{dt} = -\frac{w}{l}\frac{dl}{dt}$. When $l = 10$, $w = 5$. Given $\frac{dl}{dt} = 2$: $\frac{dw}{dt} = -\frac{5}{10}(2) = -1$ cm/s. The width is decreasing at 1 cm/s.

questions.question: The graph of $f(x)$ has a horizontal tangent at $x = -2$. What can you say about $f'(-2)$?
questions.explanation: A horizontal tangent indicates the slope of the tangent line is zero, which means $f'(-2) = 0$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered applications of derivatives.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand most derivative applications.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up problems correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review basic differentiation rules and their applications.
